passage: Latin America is known to have dozens, if not hundreds, of popular dance forms. Only five of these dances are included in international ballroom dance _______ rumba, samba, cha-cha-cha, paso doble, and jive—the last of which is grouped with the other Latin dances despite not having Latin roots.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
explanation: A comma sets off the supplementary adverb "however" from the main clause, and a colon after it correctly introduces the list of five dances that follows. A comma or semicolon can't introduce that list, and placing the semicolon after "competitions" would wrongly signal that the list contrasts with the preceding clause.
